description: Instagram Web automation playbook with verified selectors from a live logged-in session. v2.1: updated with field learnings on create flow, caption contenteditable, comment inconsistencies, and publish verification.

navigation.create_parent: The clickable element is the PARENT of svg[aria-label='New post'] — click the <a> or <div> wrapping the SVG, not the SVG itself

engagement.comment_input: textarea[aria-label='Add a comment…'], textarea[placeholder='Add a comment…'], textarea[placeholder*='comment']

create_post.trigger: svg[aria-label='New post'] (click its parent element, not the SVG)

steps: Navigate to home URL (NOT /create/select/ — it resolves to @create profile).
Find svg[aria-label='New post'] and click its PARENT element (the wrapping <a> or <div>).
Wait for file input to appear: input[type='file'].
Upload media via file input.
Click Next (first time — crop/filter step).
Click Next (second time — caption step).
Fill caption: try textarea first, fall back to div[contenteditable='true'][role='textbox'].
Click Share to publish.
VERIFY: check that URL changed to /p/{post_id}/ and caption text is rendered on page.
notes: CRITICAL: /create/select/ resolves to @create profile page — never use it as a URL.
Create trigger must be clicked precisely on the parent of svg[aria-label='New post']. Clicking broad containers won't open the composer.
Two Next clicks are required: first for crop/filter, second to reach caption/share.
Caption field can be either <textarea> or <div contenteditable> — automation must try both.
After clicking Share, verify publish by checking for post URL (/p/...) and rendered caption text. success=true alone is not reliable.

steps: Navigate to reels URL.
Use like/comment/share/save selectors on active reel.
Use follow_text selector for creator follow button.
Navigate to messages URL.
Use dm.search_input to find recipient.
Click dm.new_message if inbox is empty.
Type in dm.composer — supports both textarea and contenteditable div.
On home/reels, click engagement.comment selector.
Wait for post page/dialog to open.
Type into engagement.comment_input.
Look for Post button to submit. If no Post button found, try pressing Enter as fallback.
VERIFY: Enter fallback can fail silently — check if comment actually appeared in DOM after submit.

selectors.comment_input: textarea[aria-label='Add a comment…'], textarea[placeholder='Add a comment…'], textarea[placeholder*='comment']

notes: Comment flow is inconsistent on web — Post button may not appear.
Enter key fallback can fail silently. Always verify comment was actually posted by checking DOM.
Verified path opens https://www.instagram.com/p/... and exposes aria-label='Add a comment…'.

steps: Use follow_text (text=/^Follow$/i) on feed/reels/profile context.
Avoid class-based selectors; they are unstable.
Use semantic selectors (aria-label, text match) — this holds across all pages.
Navigate to edit_profile URL.
Set settings.website and settings.bio.
Submit via settings.submit (type='submit').
notes: Verified on Edit profile page title: 'Edit profile • Instagram'.

errors.error: /create/select/ resolves to @create profile instead of post composer
errors.solution: NEVER navigate to /create/select/. Instead, go to home page and click the parent element of svg[aria-label='New post'] in the left nav.

errors.error: Create trigger click opens nothing — composer doesn't appear
errors.context: Clicking broad container elements instead of precise create button
errors.solution: Target svg[aria-label='New post'] specifically, then click its direct parent element. Do not click outer wrapper divs.

errors.error: Caption field not found after upload
errors.context: Caption can be textarea OR contenteditable div depending on Instagram version
errors.solution: Try textarea[aria-label*='caption'] first. If not found, try div[contenteditable='true'][role='textbox'][aria-label*='caption']. For contenteditable, use browser_fill_form or Input.insertText instead of el.value=.

errors.error: Only one Next click — stuck on filter/crop screen
errors.context: Upload flow requires TWO Next clicks before reaching caption/share
errors.solution: Click Next, wait 500ms, then click Next again. First Next goes past crop/filter, second Next reaches caption step.

errors.error: Post appears published but content not actually live
errors.context: Share button click returns success but post didn't actually publish
errors.solution: After clicking Share, verify: (1) URL changed to /p/{id}/, (2) caption text is rendered in page body, (3) wait 2-3 seconds for server sync.

errors.error: Comment Post button missing — Enter key fails silently
errors.context: Comment submission on web is inconsistent
errors.solution: After typing comment, look for button with text 'Post'. If found, click it. If not, press Enter. Then VERIFY the comment appeared in DOM — Enter can fail silently.

errors.error: Notifications nav href not always present in left rail
errors.solution: Navigate directly to /notifications/ or click by aria-label/text.

errors.error: Follow button lacks stable unique CSS attributes
errors.context: Follow is a plain button with text in multiple contexts
errors.solution: Use text-based matcher: button,[role='button'] with /^Follow$/i. Never use class-based selectors.

errors.context: Instagram obfuscates CSS class names — they change frequently
errors.solution: Always use semantic selectors: aria-label, href routes, placeholder text, role attributes. These are stable across sessions.

best_practices: Always use aria-label and href selectors over class names — Instagram obfuscates classes.
For create flow: home page → svg[aria-label='New post'] parent click → file input → Next → Next → caption → Share.
Caption fields can be textarea or contenteditable — support both in automation.
Always verify post-action results (post URL, rendered text) — don't trust success flags alone.
Comment submission: prefer Post button over Enter key; verify comment appeared in DOM.
Use browser_stealth before any Instagram automation to hide webdriver flag.
Use browser_fill_form for typing (CDP keyboard events) — Instagram detects el.value= assignments.
